target.name: Halo
target.founder: Andrei Iancu, Philip Van Stockum, Charlie Rudy

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $122M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $11.0B.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Halo is a Series B semiconductor company founded by Andrei Iancu, Philip Van Stockum, and Charlie Rudy that has raised $122M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 peer companies in the Chips & Semiconductors sector—3 public (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private entities including Etched, SambaNova Systems, Positron AI, Cerebras, and d-Matrix at comparable or adjacent stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $122M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage step-ups, implying a $232M valuation. The peer-median method benchmarks against five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: SambaNova Systems ($11.0B, Series A), Etched ($5.0B, Series B), d-Matrix ($2.0B, Series C), Positron AI ($1.0B, Series B), and DEEPX ($529M, Series C). The median valuation across this cohort is $2.0B, with a range spanning $529M to $11.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $197M (floor) to $2.3B (ceiling), with a median of $1.1B. Key uncertainties center on stage-appropriate comparability—the peer set spans Series A through Series C with wide dispersion—and whether Halo's technology profile and market position align more closely with lower-quartile peers like Positron AI or higher-profile entrants like Etched.
